checked_sources: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e Ch 2.7 (Solve Linear Inequalities) reference listed in course JSON
Khan Academy Inequalities in one variable practice reference listed in course JSON
findings: Module title, order, and objective in script.json match the published Algebra I module 6 in server/prisma/courses/math/algebra-i.json: 'Inequalities & Interval Notation'.

Assignment described in the lesson (10 inequalities to solve and graph with at least 3 forcing a sign flip; 5 number-line graphs converted to interval AND set-builder notation; one-sentence-per-graph explanation of whether the endpoint is included) matches the assignment string in the source packet verbatim.

Resources referred to by name (OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e Ch 2.7 and Khan Academy inequalities practice) match the resources listed in the course JSON and the source packet; no URLs are read aloud in narration.
No AP, College Board, Common App, CEEB, NCAA, accreditation, or admissions claims appear in lesson metadata, slide text, or narration.

No sourced or generated bitmap images are used. All precision visuals — number lines, open/closed endpoint markers, shading, the interval/set-builder translation table, and both worked traces — are drawn deterministically in build_slides.py, so no assets_manifest.json is required.

Narration consistently uses 'less than', 'greater than or equal to', 'round bracket', and 'square bracket' rather than reading symbols visually, which keeps the audio track unambiguous when the slide is not visible.
